text.title: Static Application Security Testing (SAST) in CI/CD Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the SAST CI config. Each rule maps to a CI-checkable invariant.

rule.statement: PR gate MUST block only on CRITICAL / HIGH (Semgrep ERROR, CodeQL critical/high). MEDIUM and LOW are informational annotations.

rationale.source: OWASP DevSecOps Maturity, CodeQL severity guide

rule.rationale: Blocking on every severity causes alert fatigue, trains developers to `--no-verify` or bypass CI, and makes the gate worthless.

rule.statement: Scanner image / action MUST be pinned to a release tag or SHA. Rule packs MUST be pinned to a version. `@master` / floating rule packs forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent rule-pack updates flip previously-passing PRs to failing without any code change, making CI untrustworthy.

rule.statement: On first integration, baseline pre-existing findings (Semgrep `--baseline-ref`, CodeQL alert state). Gate only on new findings introduced in the PR diff.
rule.rationale: Retrofitting SAST into legacy code without baseline produces thousands of findings on first run, blocking every PR until impossible cleanup is complete.

rule.statement: Findings MUST be uploaded as SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning (or equivalent). PR comments / plain logs only are forbidden.
rule.rationale: SARIF in the Security tab is searchable, triage-able, dedup-able. PR comments scroll off; plain logs decay.

rule.statement: The SAST step MUST be a required check on the protected branch. Optional / informational-only configurations defeat the gate.
rule.rationale: If the check is optional, developers merge around it; the security signal exists but does not change behaviour.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
